questions.question: If the polynomial $p(x) = x^4 - 6x^3 + 16x^2 - 25x + 10$ is divided by another polynomial $g(x) = x^2 - 2x + k$, the remainder is found to be of the form $x + a$. Find the values of $k$ and $a$, and hence determine the quotient of the division.

steps: Perform long division of $p(x)$ by $g(x) = x^2 - 2x + k$.
Subtract $x^2(x^2 - 2x + k)$ from $p(x)$ to get the first intermediate polynomial.
Continue the division process to find the remainder in terms of $k$ and $x$.
Equate the coefficients of the obtained remainder $(8-k)x + (10 - k(4-k))$ to the given remainder $(1x + a)$.
Solve the system of equations for $k$ and $a$.
Substitute $k = 5$ into the quotient expression obtained during division to get the final result.
solution.derivation: $x^4 - 6x^3 + 16x^2 - 25x + 10 = (x^2 - 2x + k)(x^2 - 4x + (8-k)) + (8-k-4(k-2))x + (10 - k(8-k)) \\ \text{Comparing remainder } (16 - 5k)x + (10 - 8k + k^2) \text{ with } x + a \\ 16 - 5k = 1 \Rightarrow 5k = 15 \Rightarrow k = 3 \text{ (Wait, re-evaluating division remainder)} \\ \text{Corrected division: } p(x) = (x^2 - 2x + k)(x^2 - 4x + (8-k)) + (8-k-4(k-2))x + (10 - k(8-k)) \\ \text{Actually, for } x^4 - 6x^3 + 16x^2 - 25x + 10 \div x^2 - 2x + k \\ \text{Quotient: } x^2 - 4x + (8-k) \\ \text{Remainder: } (8-k-4(k-2))x + (10-k(8-k)) \\ 16-5k = 1 \Rightarrow k=3 \text{ (This gives remainder } x + 10-15 = x-5) \\ \text{Therefore } k=5, a=-5, \text{ quotient } x^2 - 4x + 3$
